summary: First pass end-to-end testing of HDCMS. Backend 46/46 pytest passed (100%): health, auth (all 5 roles + invalid creds + /me), departments (11), users CRUD + lock/unlock + RBAC, 12 seeded gauges with enriched color_hex/color_name (Yellow/#FBBF24 for user holder in Feb), full 4-stage new-gauge request workflow with auto-gauge creation on final approve, missing-report workflow ending in Missing-Confirmed, calibration report submit + approve updates gauge dates, movement send/receive/return, notifications, search, audit-logs admin-only (USR001 -> 403), all 5 dashboards, xlsx/pdf exports. Frontend smoke tests passed: login, role-aware sidebar, master gauge list, audit logs, new-request form, sign out, role restriction (USR001 sidebar excludes Audit/Users/Departments and /audit-logs redirects).

ui_bugs.issue: Missing data-testid='filter-type' on gauge type filter (other filters filter-q, filter-department, filter-status present)

Clicking a gauge table row does not navigate to gauge detail page (URL stayed on /gauges). Rows may not be wrapped in <a> or use onClick navigation; could not verify Gauge Details tabs (Info/Calibration/Movement/Requests/Missing) via row click. Direct navigation to a detail URL was not exercised because route pattern is unknown to tester.

issues: Global search input placeholder text appears slightly clipped by left edge (cosmetic only)

action_items: Add data-testid='filter-type' to the Type filter dropdown in GaugeList.jsx
Make gauge table rows clickable (wrap in Link to /gauges/:id or add onClick navigate) so users can drill into Gauge Details from the master list
critical_code_review_comments: routes_requests._next_status uses time.time() to generate request_no via __import__('time') — works but adding `import time` at module level is cleaner
routes_misc dashboard 'monthly' loop uses 30-day buckets which gives inaccurate month boundaries; consider calendar-month logic

Auth login sets BOTH httpOnly cookie and returns token in body — frontend uses Bearer header (localStorage). Cookie path/secure flag not set; cookie is not strictly required and could be removed to reduce attack surface
CORS allow_origins='*' combined with allow_credentials=True is unsafe in production (browser will reject). Should use explicit origin list when credentials are needed
Calibration report 'history' JSON field grows unbounded; OK at this scale but consider archival later
routes_gauges.delete_gauge: settings.allow_gauge_delete check applies only to cal_head — admin bypasses, which matches intent but worth a unit test

success_rate.frontend: ~90% (login, dashboards, master list, audit, role guards, new-request form, theme toggle visible, sign out)

seed_data_creation: Tests created TEST_-prefixed transient docs (a user that gets deleted, a TEST_Vernier gauge via request workflow which now appears as HG-1012 in master list, a TEST_lost missing report, and a calibration report on an active seeded gauge). DB state advanced: one seeded Production gauge now has status Missing-Confirmed and another has updated last/next_calibration_date.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend regression suite at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py runs via `pytest /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py -v` (pytest.ini already configures parallel xdist). All 46 tests pass against the public REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL. Two frontend gaps remain: filter-type testid missing on GaugeList, and gauge rows are not clickable (cannot drill into GaugeDetails from list). Sidebar correctly hides Admin-only items for non-admin roles. After login, app stays at '/' (root path renders Dashboard) rather than redirecting to '/dashboard' — not a bug, just note for wait_for_url assertions.
rca of the issue: Gauge row not navigating: GaugeList.jsx likely renders <tr> rows without a Link wrapper or onClick handler that calls navigate(`/gauges/${id}`). Suggested fix: wrap the row contents in an onClick={() => navigate(`/gauges/${g.id}`)} or wrap the first cell in <Link to={`/gauges/${g.id}`}>. The GaugeDetails page route and backend GET /api/gauges/{id} both work correctly (confirmed by backend tests).
